evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Build data consistency validation logic and DQ monitoring automation
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals (DQ monitoring, PII masking, regulatory reporting, normalization standards) saturate the JD with no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Support regulatory reporting and IFRS accounting standards
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Insurance domain data tied to regulatory reporting and IFRS 17 accounting standards is explicit financial/regulatory compliance language, placing this at the highest risk tier.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Design and operate SSOT marts for assigned domains based on DW standards
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Established SSOT/DW standards, a named Data/Infrastructure team, and IFRS 17 compliance practices indicate a mature, specialised data organisation.
evidence.jd_authorship: Apply normalization principles and DW characteristics (Subject-Oriented, Integrated, Non-Volatile, Time-Variant)
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Highly precise technical and regulatory terms (SSOT, DW characteristics, IFRS 17, Star/Snowflake schema, Airflow DAG) give a very concrete picture of the daily work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Support regulatory reporting and IFRS accounting standards
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Regulatory reporting, IFRS accounting standards, and insurance domain compliance dominate the responsibilities, a finance-oriented framing.
evidence.autonomy_level: Design and operate SSOT marts for assigned domains based on DW standards
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Designing and operating SSOT marts is ownership-oriented, but the role also frames work as partnering with developers and business teams on requests, giving mixed autonomy.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tool or AI infrastructure mention appears anywhere in the extracted JD text.
evidence.testing_framing: Build data consistency validation logic and DQ monitoring automation
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality validation and monitoring automation are named as an owned responsibility area with explicit build/ownership verbs.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Data quality directly impacts customer trust and compliance.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: The JD explicitly states data quality's link to customer trust and regulatory compliance in the team description, the clearest possible high loss-aversion framing.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design and operate SSOT marts for assigned domains based on DW standards
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role mixes designing new SSOT marts for assigned domains with operating and maintaining existing DW pipelines and quality monitoring.
evidence.loss_aversion: Data quality directly impacts customer trust and compliance. — the JD explicitly frames data quality failures as a risk to customer trust and regulatory compliance in the insurance domain.
